steps.title: Read the y-intercept of the original line from the graph
steps.content: Trace the line of best fit to where it crosses the y-axis (at $x = 0$). The line intersects the y-axis at approximately $y \approx 54.57$.

steps.title: Apply the transformation to the entire line
steps.content: When data set B is created by subtracting 9 from each $y$-value, every point shifts down by 9 units vertically. This means the entire line of best fit shifts down by exactly 9 units. If the original line has equation $y = mx + 54.57$, the new line has equation $y = mx + (54.57 - 9)$.

steps.title: Calculate the new y-intercept
steps.content:
The $y$-intercept of the line of best fit for data set B is:
$$y\text{-intercept} = 54.57 - 9 = 45.57$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
